title: rosa communis var. globata
intro: Rosa communis var. globata, also known as the wild rose or Rosa rugosa, is a shrub native to eastern Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been found to possess antibacterial and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. globata include reducing inflammation, promoting digestive health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, and salves, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
